trans-unit.source: Exercise 5: The world
trans-unit.target: Exercise 5: The world

trans-unit.source: Characteristics of the exercise
trans-unit.target: Characteristics of the exercise

trans-unit.source: :::{card} :class-card: sd-border-1 sd-shadow-none __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: :::{card} :class-card: sd-border-1 sd-shadow-none __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: This exercise will help you to get to know the interface of QGIS a bit better. Furthermore, you will load your first data in QGIS and gain some hands-on experience with the layer concept and learn how to reproject layers.
trans-unit.target: This exercise will help you to get to know the interface of QGIS a bit better. Furthermore, you will load your first data in QGIS and gain some hands-on experience with the layer concept and learn how to reproject layers.

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Type of trainings exercise:__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Type of trainings exercise:__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.
trans-unit.target: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.

trans-unit.source: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.
trans-unit.target: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.

trans-unit.source: Creating and saving a new QGIS project
trans-unit.target: Creating and saving a new QGIS project

trans-unit.source: Importing vector data (`.shp` and `.gpkg`) as well as tabular data (`.txt`)
trans-unit.target: Importing vector data (`.shp` and `.gpkg`) as well as tabular data (`.txt`)

trans-unit.source: Understanding the layer concept
trans-unit.target: Understanding the layer concept

trans-unit.source: Navigating the QGIS interface and map canvas
trans-unit.target: Navigating the QGIS interface and map canvas

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Estimated time demand for the exercise__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Estimated time demand for the exercise__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: The exercise takes around 30 minutes to complete, depending on the number of participants and trainers.
trans-unit.target: The exercise takes around 30 minutes to complete, depending on the number of participants and trainers.

trans-unit.source: [QGIS Interface](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_interface_wiki.html)
trans-unit.target: [QGIS Interface](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_interface_wiki.html)

trans-unit.source: [Types of Geodata](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geodata_types_wiki.html)
trans-unit.target: [Types of Geodata](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geodata_types_wiki.html)

trans-unit.source: [Geodata Import in QGIS](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html)
trans-unit.target: [Geodata Import in QGIS](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html)

trans-unit.source: [Layer Concept](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_layer_concept_wiki.html)
trans-unit.target: [Layer Concept](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_layer_concept_wiki.html)

trans-unit.source: Instructions for the trainers
trans-unit.target: Instructions for the trainers

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__

trans-unit.source: Prepare the training
trans-unit.target: Prepare the training

trans-unit.source: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.
trans-unit.target: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.

trans-unit.source: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.
trans-unit.target: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.

trans-unit.source: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.
trans-unit.target: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.

trans-unit.source: Check out [How to do trainings?](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.html#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on training conduction
trans-unit.target: Check out [How to do trainings?](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.html#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on training conduction

trans-unit.source: Conduct the training
trans-unit.target: Conduct the training

trans-unit.source: __Introduction:__
trans-unit.target: __Introduction:__

trans-unit.source: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.
trans-unit.target: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.

trans-unit.source: __Follow-along:__
trans-unit.target: __Follow-along:__

trans-unit.source: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.
trans-unit.target: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.

trans-unit.source: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: __Wrap up:__
trans-unit.target: __Wrap up:__

trans-unit.source: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Leave some time for open questions.
trans-unit.target: Leave some time for open questions.

trans-unit.source: Available Data
trans-unit.target: Available Data

trans-unit.source: __Download all datasets [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module_2/Module_2_Exercise_5_The_World.zip), save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__
trans-unit.target: __Download all datasets [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module_2/Module_2_Exercise_5_The_World.zip), save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__

trans-unit.source: The folder is called “Module_2_Exercise_5_The_World" and contains the whole [standard folder structure](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.md#standard-folder-structure) with all data in the input folder and the additional documentation in the documentation folder.
trans-unit.target: The folder is called “Module_2_Exercise_5_The_World" and contains the whole [standard folder structure](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.md#standard-folder-structure) with all data in the input folder and the additional documentation in the documentation folder.

trans-unit.source: [World Countries (Generalized)](https://hub.arcgis.com/datasets/2b93b06dc0dc4e809d3c8db5cb96ba69_0/explore) (Polygon/Shapefile)
trans-unit.target: [World Countries (Generalized)](https://hub.arcgis.com/datasets/2b93b06dc0dc4e809d3c8db5cb96ba69_0/explore) (Polygon/Shapefile)

trans-unit.source: [Significant Earthquake Dataset](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/metadata/landing-page/bin/iso?id=gov.noaa.ngdc.mgg.hazards:G012153) (CSV)
trans-unit.target: [Significant Earthquake Dataset](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/metadata/landing-page/bin/iso?id=gov.noaa.ngdc.mgg.hazards:G012153) (CSV)

trans-unit.source: [Global Power Plant Dataset](https://datasets.wri.org/dataset/globalpowerplantdatabase) (Points/GeoPackage)
trans-unit.target: [Global Power Plant Dataset](https://datasets.wri.org/dataset/globalpowerplantdatabase) (Points/GeoPackage)

trans-unit.source: Tasks
trans-unit.target: Tasks

trans-unit.source: Open QGIS and create a [new project](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.md#step-by-step-setting-up-a-new-qgis-project-from-scratch) by clicking on `Project` → `New`
trans-unit.target: Open QGIS and create a [new project](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.md#step-by-step-setting-up-a-new-qgis-project-from-scratch) by clicking on `Project` → `New`

trans-unit.source: Once the project is created save the project in the `/Project`-subfolder in the `/Module_2_Exercise_5_The_World`/. In the top bar, click on `Project` → `Save as` and navigate to the folder. Name the project “Module_2_Ex_5_The_World”.
trans-unit.target: Once the project is created save the project in the `/Project`-subfolder in the `/Module_2_Exercise_5_The_World`/. In the top bar, click on `Project` → `Save as` and navigate to the folder. Name the project “Module_2_Ex_5_The_World”.

trans-unit.source: Load the shape file `World_countries_generalized.shp` into your project by drag and drop ([Wiki Video](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.md#open-vector-data-via-drag-and-drop)). Or click on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`. Click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to "World_countries__generalized". Select the file and click `Open`. Back in QGIS click `Add` ([Wiki Video](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.md#open-vector-data-via-layer-tab)).
trans-unit.target: Load the shape file `World_countries_generalized.shp` into your project by drag and drop ([Wiki Video](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.md#open-vector-data-via-drag-and-drop)). Or click on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`. Click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to "World_countries__generalized". Select the file and click `Open`. Back in QGIS click `Add` ([Wiki Video](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.md#open-vector-data-via-layer-tab)).

trans-unit.source: Load the GeoPackage file `global_power_plant_database_nuclear.gpkg` into the QGIS project. You can use one of the methods used in the previous step: Either drag and drop ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-drag-and-drop)) the file or click on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`. Click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to `/data/input/`. Select the file and click `Open`. Back in QGIS click `Add`([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-layer-tab)).
trans-unit.target: Load the GeoPackage file `global_power_plant_database_nuclear.gpkg` into the QGIS project. You can use one of the methods used in the previous step: Either drag and drop ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-drag-and-drop)) the file or click on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`. Click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to `/data/input/`. Select the file and click `Open`. Back in QGIS click `Add`([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-layer-tab)).

trans-unit.source: Next, we want to load the file `Significant_earthquake_data.txt` into QGIS. Since this is vector data in text format, we need to follow specific steps ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-csv-data-in-qgis)).
trans-unit.target: Next, we want to load the file `Significant_earthquake_data.txt` into QGIS. Since this is vector data in text format, we need to follow specific steps ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-csv-data-in-qgis)).

trans-unit.source: Click on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Delimited text Layer`. Click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to `Significant_earthquake_data.txt` in the `data/input/`-subfolder. Select the file and click `Open`.
trans-unit.target: Click on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Delimited text Layer`. Click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to `Significant_earthquake_data.txt` in the `data/input/`-subfolder. Select the file and click `Open`.

trans-unit.source: In the window "Data Source manager| Delimited Text" in QGIS open the dropdown menu `File Format` and check `Custom delimiter` and `Tab`.
trans-unit.target: In the window "Data Source manager| Delimited Text" in QGIS open the dropdown menu `File Format` and check `Custom delimiter` and `Tab`.

trans-unit.source: Open the dropdown menu `Geometry definition`. Make sure the option `Point coordinates` is checked. Furthermore, select for `X field` “LONGITUDE” and for `Y field` “LATITUDE”.
trans-unit.target: Open the dropdown menu `Geometry definition`. Make sure the option `Point coordinates` is checked. Furthermore, select for `X field` “LONGITUDE” and for `Y field` “LATITUDE”.

trans-unit.source: Select the coordinate reference system (CRS) "EPSG:4326-WGS 84".
trans-unit.target: Select the coordinate reference system (CRS) "EPSG:4326-WGS 84".

trans-unit.source: `X field` =“LONGITUDE”
trans-unit.target: `X field` =“LONGITUDE”

trans-unit.source: `Y field` = “LATITUDE”. :::
trans-unit.target: `Y field` = “LATITUDE”. :::

trans-unit.source: In the layer panel on the left, arrange the three layers in a practical order. Remember the [Layer Concept](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_layer_concept_wiki.md). The countries-layer should sit below the earthquake and power plant layers.
trans-unit.target: In the layer panel on the left, arrange the three layers in a practical order. Remember the [Layer Concept](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_layer_concept_wiki.md). The countries-layer should sit below the earthquake and power plant layers.

trans-unit.source: Interact with the map and explore the data sets. Use the zoom tool and move the map. Where can you find a lot of earthquakes and where are most of the power plants located?
trans-unit.target: Interact with the map and explore the data sets. Use the zoom tool and move the map. Where can you find a lot of earthquakes and where are most of the power plants located?

trans-unit.source: Save your project by clicking on the ![](/fig/mActionFileSave.png) or use the hotkey combination <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>S</kbd>.
trans-unit.target: Save your project by clicking on the ![](/fig/mActionFileSave.png) or use the hotkey combination <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>S</kbd>.

trans-unit.source: Your results should look something like this:
trans-unit.target: Your results should look something like this:
